1718					if err := applyNoChangesSmartRetry(w.Store, candidate.ID, assignee, noChanges, report.ActualPower, w.EscalationNextFloor); err != nil {
1719						_ = commitOutcome(ctx, w.Store, candidate.ID, func() error {
1720							return commitOutcomeError("applyNoChangesSmartRetry", assignee, result, err)
1721						})
1722						if ctx.Err() != nil {
1723							return result, ctx.Err()
1724						}
1725						continue
1726					}
1727					result.Failures++
1728					result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status
1729				case agenttry.NoChangesActionOperatorRequired:
1730					if err := applyNoChangesOperatorRequired(w.Store, candidate.ID, assignee, noChanges, now().UTC()); err != nil {
1731						_ = commitOutcome(ctx, w.Store, candidate.ID, func() error {
1732							return commitOutcomeError("applyNoChangesOperatorRequired", assignee, result, err)
1733						})
1734						if ctx.Err() != nil {
1735							return result, ctx.Err()
1736						}
1737						continue
1738					}
1739					result.Failures++
1740					result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status
1741				case agenttry.NoChangesActionBlockedExternal:
1742					if err := applyNoChangesBlockedExternal(w.Store, candidate.ID, assignee, noChanges); err != nil {
1743						_ = commitOutcome(ctx, w.Store, candidate.ID, func() error {
1744							return commitOutcomeError("applyNoChangesBlockedExternal", assignee, result, err)
1745						})
1746						if ctx.Err() != nil {
1747							return result, ctx.Err()
1748						}
1749						continue
1750					}
1751					result.Failures++
1752					result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status
1753				case agenttry.NoChangesActionRetryLaterCooldown:
1754					// Unresolved: suppress immediate retry so the queue can
1755					// move on to other beads.
1756					report = w.runPostAttemptTriage(ctx, candidate, report, runtime, assignee, now)
1757					if noChanges.CooldownEligible && shouldSuppressNoProgress(report) {
1758						retryAfter := now().UTC().Add(CapLoopCooldown(noProgressCooldown))
1759						if err := w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, retryAfter, report.Status, report.Detail, report.BaseRev); err != nil {
1760							_ = commitOutcome(ctx, w.Store, candidate.ID, func() error {
1761								return commitOutcomeError("SetExecutionCooldown", assignee, result, err)
1762							})
1763							if ctx.Err() != nil {
1764								return result, ctx.Err()
1765							}
1766							continue
1767						}
1768						report.RetryAfter = retryAfter.Format(time.RFC3339)
1769					}
1770					result.Failures++
1771					result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status
1772				default:
1773					report = w.runPostAttemptTriage(ctx, candidate, report, runtime, assignee, now)
1774					result.Failures++
1775					result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status
1776				}
1777			} else {
1778				if attemptOut.Parking == nil && attemptOut.Disposition != agenttry.OutcomePark {
1779					if err := w.Store.Unclaim(candidate.ID); err != nil {
1780						_ = commitOutcome(ctx, w.Store, candidate.ID, func() error {
1781							return commitOutcomeError("Unclaim", assignee, result, err)
1782						})
1783						if ctx.Err() != nil {
1784							return result, ctx.Err()
1785						}
1786						continue
1787					}
1788				}
1789				if report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusPreservedNeedsReview {
1790					if err := w.Store.AppendNotes(candidate.ID, preservedNeedsReviewNote(report)); err != nil {
1791						_ = commitOutcome(ctx, w.Store, candidate.ID, func() error {
1792							return commitOutcomeError("AppendNotes", assignee, result, err)
1793						})
1794						if ctx.Err() != nil {
1795							return result, ctx.Err()
1796						}
1797						continue
1798					}
1799					result.Failures++
1800					result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status
1801				} else if report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusRepairCycleExhausted {
1802					if err := applyRepairCycleExhaustedEscalation(w.Store, candidate.ID, assignee, report.ActualPower, now(), w.EscalationNextFloor); err != nil {
1803						_ = commitOutcome(ctx, w.Store, candidate.ID, func() error {
1804							return commitOutcomeError("applyRepairCycleExhaustedEscalation", assignee, result, err)
1805						})
1806						if ctx.Err() != nil {
1807							return result, ctx.Err()
1808						}
1809						continue
1810					}
1811					result.Failures++
1812					result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status
1813				} else {
1814					if isNoViableProviderReport(report) {
1815						report.OutcomeReason = FailureModeNoViableProvider
1816						retryAfter := now().UTC().Add(CapLoopCooldown(ProviderUnavailableCooldown))
1817						if err := w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, retryAfter, report.Status, report.Detail, report.BaseRev); err != nil {
1818							_ = commitOutcome(ctx, w.Store, candidate.ID, func() error {
1819								return commitOutcomeError("SetExecutionCooldown", assignee, result, err)
1820							})
1821							if ctx.Err() != nil {
1822								return result, ctx.Err()
1823							}
1824							continue
1825						}
1826						report.RetryAfter = retryAfter.Format(time.RFC3339)
1827					} else {
1828						report = w.runPostAttemptTriage(ctx, candidate, report, runtime, assignee, now)
1829						if shouldSuppressNoProgress(report) {
1830							retryAfter := now().UTC().Add(CapLoopCooldown(noProgressCooldown))
1831							if err := w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, retryAfter, report.Status, report.Detail, report.BaseRev); err != nil {
1832								_ = commitOutcome(ctx, w.Store, candidate.ID, func() error {
1833									return commitOutcomeError("SetExecutionCooldown", assignee, result, err)
1834								})
1835								if ctx.Err() != nil {
1836									return result, ctx.Err()
1837								}
1838								continue
1839							}
1840							report.RetryAfter = retryAfter.Format(time.RFC3339)
1841						}
1842					}
1843					result.Failures++
1844					result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status
1845				}
1846			}
1847	
1848			result.Results = append(result.Results, report)
1849			callAttemptFinalized(runtime.OnAttemptFinalized, report)
1850	
1851			// Skip the late execute-bead append for already-satisfied beads —
1852			// the satisfied path appends its own terminal event before
1853			// CloseWithEvidence so the closure gate sees execution evidence.
1854			// Duplicating it here would yield two identical events.
1855			if report.Status != ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied {
1856				if err := w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, executeBeadLoopEvent(report, assignee, now().UTC())); err != nil {
1857					_ = commitOutcome(ctx, w.Store, candidate.ID, func() error {
1858						return commitOutcomeError("AppendEvent", assignee, result, err)
1859					})
1860					// Event recording failure is non-terminal: log it and continue.
1861					// result counters were already updated by the outcome block above;
1862					// do not double-count by re-running the outcome handler.
1863					if runtime.Log != nil {
1864						_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "outcome store error (AppendEvent %s): %v (continuing)\n", candidate.ID, err)
1865					}
1866					if ctx.Err() == nil {
1867						_ = w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, now().UTC().Add(StoreErrorCooldown), "loop-error", "AppendEvent: "+err.Error(), "")
1868					}
1869					if ctx.Err() != nil {
1870						return result, ctx.Err()
1871					}
1872					continue
1873				}
1874			}
1875	
1876			// Emit terminal progress phase event.
1877			terminalPhase := "failed"
1878			if report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess || report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied {
1879				terminalPhase = "done"
1880			} else if report.PreserveRef != "" {
1881				terminalPhase = "preserved"
1882			}
1883			// Use the real attempt_id from the report if available.
1884			finalAttemptID := report.AttemptID
1885			if finalAttemptID == "" {
1886				finalAttemptID = provAttemptID
1887			}
1888			phaseSeq++
1889			emitProgress(runtime.ProgressCh, ProgressEvent{
1890				EventID:   "evt-" + randomProgressID(),
1891				AttemptID: finalAttemptID,
1892				WorkerID:  runtime.WorkerID,
1893				ProjectID: runtime.ProjectRoot,
1894				BeadID:    candidate.ID,
1895				Harness:   harness,
1896				Model:     model,
1897				Profile:   profile,
1898				Phase:     terminalPhase,
1899				PhaseSeq:  phaseSeq,
1900				Heartbeat: false,
1901				TS:        now().UTC(),
1902				ElapsedMS: now().Sub(runStart).Milliseconds(),
1903				Message:   report.Detail,
1904			})
1905	
1906			emit("bead.result", map[string]any{
1907				"bead_id":              candidate.ID,
1908				"status":               report.Status,
1909				"detail":               report.Detail,
1910				"session_id":           report.SessionID,
1911				"result_rev":           report.ResultRev,
1912				"base_rev":             report.BaseRev,
1913				"preserve_ref":         report.PreserveRef,
1914				"no_changes_rationale": report.NoChangesRationale,
1915				"duration_ms":          now().Sub(runStart).Milliseconds(),
1916			})
1917	
1918			if runtime.Log != nil {
1919				_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(runtime.Log, formatLoopResultLine(candidate.ID, report))
1920			}
1921	
1922			if loopMode == executeloop.ModeOnce {
1923				applyStop(work.StopInput{Once: true})
1924				return result, nil
1925			}
1926		}
1927	}

1972	// emitPickerPrioritySkips fires a picker.priority_skip event when the picker
1973	// chose `chosen` while at least one strictly higher-priority bead was passed
1974	// over (ddx-9d55601f AC #4). Skips at the same priority as `chosen` are NOT
1975	// reported — those are FIFO order or label-filter siblings, not starvation.
1976	//
1977	// The emit is guarded so it costs nothing when the chosen bead is the highest-
1978	// priority bead the picker saw (the common case).
1979	func emitPickerPrioritySkips(emit func(string, map[string]any), chosen bead.Bead, skips []pickerSkip) {
1980		if len(skips) == 0 {
1981			return
1982		}
1983		var higher []map[string]any
1984		for _, s := range skips {
1985			if s.Priority < chosen.Priority {
1986				higher = append(higher, map[string]any{
1987					"bead_id":    s.BeadID,
1988					"priority":   s.Priority,
1989					"queue_rank": queueRankValue(s.BeadRank),
1990					"reason":     s.Reason,
1991				})
1992			}
1993		}
1994		if len(higher) == 0 {
1995			return
1996		}
1997		emit("picker.priority_skip", map[string]any{
1998			"chosen_bead_id":  chosen.ID,
1999			"chosen_priority": chosen.Priority,
2000			"skipped":         higher,
2001		})
2002	}
2003	
2004	// pickerSkip records a single bead that the picker passed over while looking
2005	// for the next candidate. It is used to emit a structured picker.priority_skip
2006	// event when a worker claims a lower-priority bead while a higher-priority
2007	// bead was skipped, so future starvation regressions are observable.
2008	//
2009	// Reason values are bounded to the set named in ddx-9d55601f AC #4:
2010	//
2011	//	label_filter, in_attempted, claim_race, eligibility_filter, retry_cooldown
2012	//
2013	// Note: eligibility_filter and retry_cooldown are applied upstream in
2014	// Store.ReadyExecution (so beads filtered for those reasons never reach
2015	// nextCandidate at all). They are part of the reason vocabulary so future
2016	// picker rearrangements can re-emit them without changing the schema.
2017	type pickerSkip struct {
2018		BeadID   string
2019		Priority int
2020		BeadRank *int
2021		Reason   string
2022	}
2023	
2024	func hasGuardSkips(skips []pickerSkip) bool {
2025		for _, skip := range skips {
2026			switch skip.Reason {
2027			case "label_filter", "in_attempted", "claim_race", "eligibility_filter", "retry_cooldown", "target_bead":
2028				continue
2029			default:
2030				return true
2031			}
2032		}
2033		return false
2034	}
2035	
2036	func queueRankValue(raw any) any {
2037		rank, ok := parseQueueRank(raw)
2038		if !ok {
2039			return nil
2040		}
2041		return rank
2042	}
2043	
2044	// nextCandidate returns the next claimable bead from the execution-ready
2045	// queue along with the list of higher-priority beads it skipped (and the
2046	// reason for each skip). The returned skips slice is only meaningful when
2047	// ok=true: it contains every entry that came BEFORE the chosen candidate
2048	// in the priority-sorted ReadyExecution result.
2049	//
2050	// It delegates filter+sort to PreviewQueue and then additionally filters out
2051	// beads already present in the current drain results slice (which is
2052	// non-deterministic across runs and therefore excluded from the stable
2053	// PreviewQueue surface).
2054	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) nextCandidate(ctx context.Context, results []ExecuteBeadReport, guards []work.Guard, labelFilter, targetBeadID string) (bead.Bead, []pickerSkip, bool, error) {
2055		// Use PreviewQueue for the stable filter+sort logic. Limit=0 returns all
2056		// entries so we can scan for the first candidate not already present in
2057		// the current drain results slice.
2058		entries, err := PreviewQueue(w.Store, PickerFilters{LabelFilter: labelFilter}, 0)
2059		if err != nil {
2060			return bead.Bead{}, nil, false, err
2061		}
2062	
2063		// Rebuild the ready list from the preview entries in picker order so we
2064		// can apply the per-run drain results slice on top. We need the original
2065		// bead values for the return; fetch them from ReadyExecution (already
2066		// ordered).
2067		ready, err := w.Store.ReadyExecution()
2068		if err != nil {
2069			return bead.Bead{}, nil, false, err
2070		}
2071		// Index by ID for O(1) lookup.
2072		byID := make(map[string]bead.Bead, len(ready))
2073		for _, b := range ready {
2074			byID[b.ID] = b
2075		}
2076		var skips []pickerSkip
2077		for _, entry := range entries {
2078			candidate, ok := byID[entry.BeadID]
2079			if !ok {
2080				// Should not happen; skip defensively.
2081				continue
2082			}
2083			if targetBeadID != "" && candidate.ID != targetBeadID {
2084				skips = append(skips, pickerSkip{BeadID: candidate.ID, Priority: candidate.Priority, BeadRank: queueRankPtr(candidate.Extra["queue-rank"]), Reason: "target_bead"})
2085				continue
2086			}
2087			if hasResultForBead(results, candidate.ID) {
2088				skips = append(skips, pickerSkip{BeadID: candidate.ID, Priority: candidate.Priority, BeadRank: queueRankPtr(candidate.Extra["queue-rank"]), Reason: "in_attempted"})
2089				continue
2090			}
2091			if entry.FilterDecision == FilterDecisionSkipped {
2092				// PreviewQueue already applied label_filter; record as skip.
2093				skips = append(skips, pickerSkip{BeadID: candidate.ID, Priority: candidate.Priority, BeadRank: queueRankPtr(candidate.Extra["queue-rank"]), Reason: "label_filter"})
2094				continue
2095			}
2096			allowed := true
2097			for _, guard := range guards {
2098				if guard == nil {
2099					continue
2100				}
2101				ok, reason := guard.Allow(ctx, candidate.ID)
2102				if ok {
2103					continue
2104				}
2105				allowed = false
2106				if reason != "" {
2107					skips = append(skips, pickerSkip{BeadID: candidate.ID, Priority: candidate.Priority, BeadRank: queueRankPtr(candidate.Extra["queue-rank"]), Reason: reason})
2108				}
2109				break
2110			}
2111			if !allowed {
2112				continue
2113			}
2114			return candidate, skips, true, nil
2115		}
2116		return bead.Bead{}, skips, false, nil
2117	}

2119	func hasResultForBead(results []ExecuteBeadReport, beadID string) bool {
2120		for _, report := range results {
2121			if report.BeadID == beadID {
2122				return true
2123			}
2124		}
2125		return false
2126	}

2128	// appendLoopRoutingEvidence records a kind:routing evidence event on the bead
2129	// from the executor's ExecuteBeadReport, so that review-outcomes analytics can
2130	// attribute a subsequent review verdict to the originating provider/model tier.
2131	// Best-effort: errors and missing-provider cases are silently ignored.
2132	func appendLoopRoutingEvidence(store BeadEventAppender, beadID string, report ExecuteBeadReport, createdAt time.Time) {
2133		if store == nil || beadID == "" {
2134			return
2135		}
2136		provider := report.Provider
2137		if provider == "" {
2138			provider = report.Harness
2139		}
2140		if provider == "" {
2141			return
2142		}
2143		body, err := json.Marshal(map[string]any{
2144			"resolved_provider": provider,

2147			"requested_profile": report.RequestedProfile,
2148			"requested_tier":    report.RequestedTier,

2150			"escalation_count":  report.EscalationCount,
2151			"final_tier":        report.FinalTier,
2152		})
2153		if err != nil {
2154			return
2155		}
2156		summary := "provider=" + provider
2157		if report.Model != "" {
2158			summary += " model=" + report.Model
2159		}
2160		_ = store.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
2161			Kind:      "routing",
2162			Summary:   summary,
2163			Body:      string(body),
2164			Actor:     "ddx",
2165			Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
2166			CreatedAt: createdAt,
2167		})
2168	}

2228		if store == nil || beadID == "" {

2234			"suggested_fixes": result.SuggestedFixes,
2235			"waivers_applied": result.WaiversApplied,
2236		}
2237		summary := fmt.Sprintf("score=%d", result.Score)
2238		if threshold > 0 {

2240		}
2241		if lintErr != nil {

2247		if encoded, err := json.Marshal(body); err == nil {

2249				Kind:      "bead-quality.lint",

2254				CreatedAt: createdAt,
2255			})
2256			return
2257		}
2258		parts := []string{
2259			fmt.Sprintf("score=%d", result.Score),

### 1. **preclaim_intake.go** (Complete file - 94 lines)

This file defines the pre-claim intake decision logic for beads. Key components include:

- **PreClaimIntakeOutcome enum**: Classifies readiness outcomes - `actionable_atomic`, `actionable_but_rewritten`, `too_large_decomposed`, `operator_required`, `intake_error`

- **PreClaimIntakeResult struct**: Contains the decision outcome, reason, system reason, detail, rewrite operations, and decomposition information

- **PreClaimDecomposition**: Holds splitter output when a bead is too large - includes children specs and an AC (acceptance criteria) map

- **isDecompositionLossy()**: Helper function to validate AC map completeness - returns true if any entry has empty Coverage

- **PreClaimIntakeHook**: Function type for intake classification - runs after routing preflight and before Claim

- **normalizedOutcome()**: Normalizes and validates PreClaimIntakeOutcome values

### 2. **execute_bead_loop.go** (Large file - 2500+ lines, partial read)

This is the core bead execution loop orchestrator. Key sections include:

- **ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime struct**: Carries non-serializable runtime plumbing for the loop, including hooks (PreClaimIntakeHook, PreDispatchLintHook, PostAttemptTriageHook), resource checking, cleanup runners, and event/progress channels

- **ExecuteBeadLoopResult**: Captures loop outcome with attempt counts, success/failure tallies, stop condition, and per-bead reports

- **ExecuteBeadReport struct**: Comprehensive attempt result including bead ID, model/harness/provider info, status, routing details, disruption tracking, and outcome classification

- **ExecuteBeadWorker**: Main worker struct that orchestrates the drain loop with Store, Executor, SatisfactionChecker, Reviewer, and optional ConflictResolver

- **Run() method** (lines 635-1927): The main execution loop that:
  - Initializes runtime state and complexity guards
  - Iterates through ready beads from the queue
  - Applies pre-claim intake hooks to classify actionability
  - Handles decomposition when beads are too large
  - Executes attempts via the Executor with heartbeat tracking
  - Manages outcome handling (success/failure/no-changes)
  - Applies post-attempt triage when enabled
  - Sets cooldowns for retry after transient failures
  - Handles disruption classification for interrupted attempts

- **Key lifecycle flow**:
  1. Claim atomically
  2. Run pre-claim intake hook (readiness check)
  3. Run pre-dispatch lint hook (quality check)
  4. Execute via `agenttry.Attempt` with conflict recovery
  5. Run post-attempt triage hook
  6. Determine outcome (success/failure/park/no-changes/etc.)
  7. Set cooldown if needed
  8. Emit progress and event stream

132	// MaxLoopCooldown because land conflicts often resolve quickly as sibling beads
133	// advance the base branch.
134	const LandConflictCooldown = 15 * time.Minute
135	
136	// StoreErrorCooldown is the short cooldown applied when a Store.* operation in
137	// the outcome-handling block returns a transient error. It prevents an
138	// immediate re-queue of the affected bead while allowing the loop to continue
139	// to the next candidate. Store errors during outcome handling are non-fatal:
140	// they are logged and emitted as loop-error events but do not terminate the
141	// worker.
142	const StoreErrorCooldown = 5 * time.Minute
143	
144	// ProviderUnavailableCooldown is the retry pause for route/provider outages
145	// where no configured tier can currently run the bead. This is transient
146	// scheduler state, not bead quality evidence, so it must not become a durable
147	// triage:needs-investigation park.
148	const ProviderUnavailableCooldown = 15 * time.Minute
149	
150	// SubmitWithPreMergeChecks is the canonical land-back step for the execute-bead
151	// loop. It runs the project's .ddx/checks/*.yaml gate against the worker's
152	// (baseRev, resultRev) before forwarding to submit. On Blocked, it preserves
153	// the worker's result under refs/ddx/iterations/, records checks-blocked
154	// events on the bead, and returns the synthesised LandResult{Status:"preserved"}
155	// without ever calling submit. On pass, honoured checks-bypass annotations are
156	// recorded as checks-bypass events and submit runs normally.
157	//
158	// submit is the project's land coordinator entry point (typically
159	// LocalLandCoordinator.Submit or the server-side coordinator submit).
160	//
161	// The implementation lives in checks_premerge.go alongside its supporting
162	// helpers (RunPreMergeChecks, AppendPreMergeChecksEvents,
163	// PreserveAfterPreMergeChecks); this thin wrapper exists so the execute-bead
164	// loop's land-back call site is one obvious helper rather than three nested
165	// ones, and so callers wiring the gate do not need to import the helper layer
166	// directly.
167	func SubmitWithPreMergeChecks(
168		ctx context.Context,
169		projectRoot string,
170		b *bead.Bead,
171		res *ExecuteBeadResult,
172		submit func(LandRequest) (*LandResult, error),
173		eventStore BeadEventAppender,
174		actor, source string,
175		now func() time.Time,
176	) (*LandResult, *PreMergeChecksOutcome, error) {
177		if now == nil {
178			now = time.Now
179		}
180		if submit == nil {
181			return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("submit-with-pre-merge-checks: submit callback required")
182		}
183		evidenceDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, res.ExecutionDir)
184		outcome, err := RunPreMergeChecks(ctx, projectRoot, b, res.BaseRev, res.ResultRev, evidenceDir)
185		if err != nil {
186			// Treat checks_bypass / loader errors as a hard preserve: the worker
187			// did its job; the operator misconfigured the gate. Better to park
188			// the result under an iteration ref than to silently land it.
189			ref := PreserveRef(res.BeadID, res.BaseRev)
190			if upErr := (&RealOrchestratorGitOps{}).UpdateRef(projectRoot, ref, res.ResultRev); upErr != nil {
191				return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("preserving result after checks setup error: %w (original error: %v)", upErr, err)
192			}
193			return &LandResult{
194				Status:      "preserved",
195				PreserveRef: ref,
196				Reason:      "pre-merge checks setup error: " + err.Error(),
197			}, nil, nil
198		}
199		if outcome.Blocked {
200			land, perr := PreserveAfterPreMergeChecks(projectRoot, res, outcome, &RealOrchestratorGitOps{})
201			if perr != nil {
202				return nil, outcome, perr
203			}
204			if eventStore != nil {
205				_ = AppendPreMergeChecksEvents(eventStore, res.BeadID, outcome, actor, source, now().UTC())
206			}
207			return land, outcome, nil
208		}
209		if eventStore != nil && len(outcome.Bypassed) > 0 {
210			_ = AppendPreMergeChecksEvents(eventStore, res.BeadID, outcome, actor, source, now().UTC())
211		}
212		land, sErr := submit(BuildLandRequestFromResult(projectRoot, res))
213		return land, outcome, sErr
214	}

216	// handlePostAttemptDecomposition runs the orchestrator-level splitter when a
217	// no_changes attempt signals orchestrator_action: decompose. It checks the
218	// queue-level max_decomposition_depth (not the implementation prompt cap),
219	// validates the AC map for completeness, and either creates children+deps or
220	// parks the parent for operator review if the split is lossy or depth-capped.
221	// The bead must already be unclaimed before this is called.
222	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) handlePostAttemptDecomposition(ctx context.Context, candidate *bead.Bead, runtime ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime, assignee string, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, at time.Time) {
223		emit := func(kind string, body map[string]any) {
224			if runtime.EventSink == nil {
225				return
226			}
227			data, _ := json.Marshal(map[string]any{"event": kind, "payload": body})
228			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.EventSink, "%s\n", data)
229		}
230		parkOperator := func(reason string) {
231			if runtime.Log != nil {
232				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "post-attempt decomposition blocked: %s (%s)\n", reason, candidate.ID)
233			}
234			emit("post_attempt_decomposition.blocked", map[string]any{
235				"bead_id": candidate.ID,
236				"reason":  reason,
237			})
238			if berr := parkBeadPostIntakeRejection(w.Store, candidate.ID, assignee, PreClaimIntakeOperatorRequired, reason, at); berr != nil && runtime.Log != nil {
239				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "post-attempt decomposition park error: %v\n", berr)
240			}
241		}
242	
243		// Queue-level depth check: orchestrator uses its own cap, not the
244		// implementation prompt's hardcoded depth-2 cap.
245		maxDepth := rcfg.MaxDecompositionDepth()
246		if maxDepth > 0 && storeBeadDepth(w.Store, candidate) >= maxDepth {
247			overflowBody, _ := json.Marshal(map[string]any{
248				"depth": storeBeadDepth(w.Store, candidate),
249				"max":   maxDepth,
250			})
251			_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
252				Kind:      "triage-overflow",
253				Summary:   "depth cap exceeded during post-attempt decomposition",
254				Body:      string(overflowBody),
255				Actor:     assignee,
256				Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
257				CreatedAt: at,
258			})
259			parkOperator("queue-level depth cap exceeded; operator must split")
260			return
261		}
262	
263		decomp, err := runtime.PostAttemptDecompositionHook(ctx, candidate.ID)
264		if err != nil {
265			parkOperator(fmt.Sprintf("decomposition hook error: %s", err.Error()))
266			return
267		}
268		if decomp == nil {
269			parkOperator("decomposition hook returned no split")
270			return
271		}
272		lossyOrEmpty := isDecompositionLossy(decomp.ACMap) || (len(decomp.ACMap) == 0 && strings.TrimSpace(candidate.Acceptance) != "")
273		if lossyOrEmpty {
274			parkOperator("decomposition AC map is incomplete; operator must produce a lossless split")
275			return
276		}
277	
278		childIDs, decompErr := applyPreClaimDecomposition(w.Store, candidate, decomp, assignee, at)
279		if decompErr != nil {
280			parkOperator(fmt.Sprintf("decomposition apply error: %s", decompErr.Error()))
281			return
282		}
283		if runtime.Log != nil {
284			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "post-attempt decomposition: bead %s split into %s\n", candidate.ID, strings.Join(childIDs, ", "))
285		}
286		emit("post_attempt_decomposition.applied", map[string]any{
287			"bead_id":   candidate.ID,
288			"child_ids": childIDs,
289		})
290	}

292	// runPostAttemptTriage invokes the optional post-attempt triage hook after
293	// the attempt outcome has been finalized but before any cooldown decision is
294	// applied. Hook failures are fail-open: the report is returned unchanged and
295	// legacy cooldown behavior remains in force.
296	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) runPostAttemptTriage(ctx context.Context, candidate bead.Bead, report ExecuteBeadReport, runtime ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime, assignee string, now func() time.Time) ExecuteBeadReport {
297		hook := runtime.PostAttemptTriageHook
298		if hook == nil {
299			return report
300		}
301		if isDeterministicSystemOutcomeReason(report.OutcomeReason) {
302			return report
303		}
304		triage, err := hook(ctx, candidate.ID, report)
305		if err != nil {
306			if runtime.Log != nil {
307				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "post-attempt triage error (%s %s): %v (continuing)\n", candidate.ID, report.Status, err)
308			}
309			return report
310		}
311		triage = normalizeTriageResult(triage)
312		hasWarnings := len(triage.DecodeWarnings) > 0 || triage.Malformed
313		classificationRecognized := isRecognizedTriageClassification(triage.Classification)
314		if hasWarnings && runtime.Log != nil {
315			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "post-attempt triage warning (%s %s): %s (continuing)\n", candidate.ID, report.Status, formatTriageWarnings(triage.DecodeWarnings))
316		}
317		if !classificationRecognized && !hasWarnings {
318			return report
319		}
320		if classificationRecognized {
321			report.OutcomeReason = triage.Classification
322		}
323		recordPostAttemptTriageEvent(w.Store, candidate.ID, report, triage, assignee, now().UTC())
324		return report
325	}

327	// CapLoopCooldown clamps a loop-set cooldown duration to MaxLoopCooldown.
328	// The loop uses this for every SetExecutionCooldown call so no automatic
329	// path can silently park a bead for a year.
330	func CapLoopCooldown(d time.Duration) time.Duration {
331		if d <= 0 {
332			return 0
333		}
334		if d > MaxLoopCooldown {
335			return MaxLoopCooldown
336		}
337		return d
338	}

561	// ExecuteBeadWorker drains the current single-project execution-ready queue.
562	// It intentionally does not retry a failed/conflicted bead again in the same
563	// process run; a later operator-driven invocation can create the next attempt.
564	type ExecuteBeadWorker struct {
565		Store               ExecuteBeadLoopStore
566		Executor            ExecuteBeadExecutor
567		SatisfactionChecker agenttry.SatisfactionChecker // nil → NoChangesContract default
568		// VerificationRunner runs a verification_command parsed out of a
569		// no_changes_rationale.txt under NoChangesContract (TD-031 §8.1). When
570		// nil, DefaultVerificationCommandRunner is used.
571		VerificationRunner agenttry.VerificationCommandRunner
572		Now                func() time.Time
573		// Reviewer is retained for legacy/manual review helper tests. Automated
574		// close eligibility is owned by pre-land candidate-cycle review, so the
575		// execute-loop success path no longer calls this post-land reviewer.
576		Reviewer BeadReviewer
577		// ComplexityGate, when non-nil, is consulted before a bead is claimed.
578		// The zero value fail-opens once and then allows.
579		ComplexityGate work.Guard
580	
581		// ConflictResolver, when non-nil, is called after the 3-way ort auto-merge
582		// step fails to recover a preserved iteration. The implementation should
583		// attempt a focused conflict-resolution pass (e.g. a cheap-tier agent run
584		// with the conflict files and bead AC) and return the new merged tip SHA on
585		// success. isBlocking=true signals the conflict requires human judgment
586		// (escalating to land_conflict_operator_required); false means failed-but-retriable.
587		ConflictResolver func(ctx context.Context, beadID, preserveRef, projectRoot string) (newTip string, isBlocking bool, err error)
588	
589		// EscalationNextFloor, when non-nil, is called by the no-changes smart-retry
590		// path to advance the MinPower floor by exactly one ladder step above
591		// actualPower. Returns an error (e.g. ErrLadderExhausted) when already at the
592		// top tier, causing the bead to be parked to status=proposed.
593		EscalationNextFloor func(actualPower int) (int, error)
594	
595		// conflictAutoRecoverFn replaces the default landConflictAutoRecover. Set
596		// in tests to inject controlled recovery results without a real git repo.
597		conflictAutoRecoverFn func(wd, preserveRef string, gitOps LandingGitOps) (string, error)
598	}

600	// emitProgress sends a ProgressEvent to runtime.ProgressCh non-blocking.
601	// If ch is nil or full the event is silently dropped.
602	func emitProgress(ch chan<- ProgressEvent, evt ProgressEvent) {
603		if ch == nil {
604			return
605		}
606		select {
607		case ch <- evt:
608		default:
609		}
610	}

612	// newProgressEvent builds a ProgressEvent with a random event_id and current timestamp.
613	func newProgressEvent(workerID, projectID, beadID, attemptID, harness, model, profile, phase string, phaseSeq int, heartbeat bool, elapsedMS int64) ProgressEvent {
614		return ProgressEvent{
615			EventID:   "evt-" + randomProgressID(),
616			AttemptID: attemptID,
617			WorkerID:  workerID,
618			ProjectID: projectID,
619			BeadID:    beadID,
620			Harness:   harness,
621			Model:     model,
622			Profile:   profile,
623			Phase:     phase,
624			PhaseSeq:  phaseSeq,
625			Heartbeat: heartbeat,
626			TS:        time.Now().UTC(),
627			ElapsedMS: elapsedMS,
628		}
629	}
630	
631	func randomProgressID() string {
632		return fmt.Sprintf("%x", time.Now().UnixNano())[:8]
633	}

635	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) Run(ctx context.Context, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime) (*ExecuteBeadLoopResult, error) {
636		if w.Store == nil {
637			return nil, fmt.Errorf("execute-bead loop: store is required")
638		}
639		if w.Executor == nil {
640			return nil, fmt.Errorf("execute-bead loop: executor is required")
641		}
642	
643		now := w.Now
644		if now == nil {
645			now = time.Now
646		}
647		assignee := rcfg.Assignee()
648		if assignee == "" {
649			assignee = "ddx"
650		}
651		noProgressCooldown := rcfg.NoProgressCooldown()
652		if noProgressCooldown <= 0 {
653			noProgressCooldown = 6 * time.Hour
654		}
655		heartbeatInterval := rcfg.HeartbeatInterval()
656		if heartbeatInterval <= 0 {
657			heartbeatInterval = bead.HeartbeatInterval
658		}
659		harness := rcfg.Harness()
660		provider := rcfg.Provider()
661		model := rcfg.Model()
662		profile := rcfg.Profile()
663		loopMode, idleInterval := runtime.loopIntent()
664	
665		result := &ExecuteBeadLoopResult{}
666		resultsResetIdx := 0
667		complexityGuard := work.NewComplexityGuard(w.ComplexityGate, runtime.Log)
668		preclaimGuard := work.NewPreClaimGuard(runtime.PreClaimHook, w.Store, runtime.Log, now, 30*time.Second)
669		workLog := NewWorkLogRenderer(WorkLogRendererOptions{Now: now})
670		wasIdle := false
671		lastIdleQueueSignature := ""
672	
673		emit := func(eventType string, data map[string]any) {
674			writeLoopEvent(runtime.EventSink, runtime.SessionID, eventType, data, now().UTC())
675		}
676	
677		emit("loop.start", map[string]any{
678			"worker_id":    runtime.WorkerID,
679			"project_root": runtime.ProjectRoot,
680			"harness":      harness,
681			"model":        model,
682			"session_id":   runtime.SessionID,
683			"assignee":     assignee,
684			"mode":         string(loopMode),
685			"once":         loopMode == executeloop.ModeOnce,
686		})
687		cleanupLog := runtime.CleanupLog
688		if cleanupLog == nil {
689			cleanupLog = runtime.Log
690		}
691		cleanupStop := startExecutionCleanupWorker(ctx, runtime.ProjectRoot, runtime.CleanupRunner, runtime.CleanupInterval, runtime.CleanupTickCh, cleanupLog, emit)
692		defer cleanupStop()
693		_, _, _ = runExecutionCleanupPass(ctx, runtime.ProjectRoot, runtime.CleanupRunner, cleanupLog, emit, "startup")
694		// exitReason is populated as the loop exits to surface a structured reason
695		// in the loop.end event (ddx-dc157075 AC #4). Work-owned terminal reasons
696		// are classified through work.StopCondition; fatal_config,
697		// preflight_failed, resource_exhausted, and future provider exhaustion are
698		// still subsystem-specific exits.
699		exitReason := ""
700		setExit := func(condition, reason string) {
701			exitReason = reason
702			result.StopCondition = condition
703			result.ExitReason = reason
704		}
705		applyStop := func(input work.StopInput) bool {
706			decision, ok := work.ClassifyStop(input)
707			if !ok {
708				return false
709			}
710			setExit(string(decision.Condition), decision.ExitReason)
711			return true
712		}
713		if runtime.RoutePreflight != nil {
714			if rerr := runtime.RoutePreflight(ctx, harness, model); rerr != nil {
715				detail := fmt.Sprintf("routing preflight rejected (harness=%s model=%s): %s",
716					harness, model, rerr.Error())
717				if runtime.Log != nil {
718					_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "routing preflight: %s (startup)\n", detail)
719				}
720				emit("preflight.rejected", map[string]any{
721					"harness": harness,
722					"model":   model,
723					"reason":  rerr.Error(),
724					"startup": true,
725				})
726				report := ExecuteBeadReport{
727					Status:           ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
728					Detail:           detail,
729					Harness:          harness,
730					Model:            model,
731					Disrupted:        true,
732					DisruptionReason: "preflight_rejected",
733					OutcomeReason:    "preflight_failed",
734				}
735				emitDisruptionDetected(emit, w.Store, "", "preflight_rejected", detail, harness, model, assignee, now().UTC())
736				result.Failures++
737				result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status
738				result.Results = append(result.Results, report)
739				setExit("Preflight", "preflight_failed")
740				return result, nil
741			}
742		}
743		defer func() {
744			emit("loop.end", map[string]any{
745				"attempts":            result.Attempts,
746				"successes":           result.Successes,
747				"failures":            result.Failures,
748				"last_failure_status": result.LastFailureStatus,
749				"exit_reason":         exitReason,
750			})
751		}()
752	
753		for {
754			_, _, _ = runExecutionCleanupPass(ctx, runtime.ProjectRoot, runtime.CleanupRunner, cleanupLog, emit, "pre-claim")
755			// Respect context cancellation between iterations. Without this check,
756			// a Stop() request (which cancels ctx) would only take effect during
757			// the idle poll sleep — the loop would happily claim the next ready
758			// bead as soon as the current Execute returned, ignoring the cancel.
759			if err := ctx.Err(); err != nil {
760				if exitReason == "" {
761					applyStop(work.StopInput{ContextErr: err})
762				}
763				return result, err
764			}
765			if runtime.BudgetStop != nil {
766				if budgetReport, stopped := runtime.BudgetStop(); stopped {
767					applyStop(work.StopInput{Budget: true})
768					if budgetReport.Status == "" {
769						budgetReport.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
770					}
771					if budgetReport.Detail != "" && runtime.Log != nil {
772						_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(runtime.Log, budgetReport.Detail)
773					}
774					result.Failures++
775					result.LastFailureStatus = budgetReport.Status
776					result.Results = append(result.Results, budgetReport)
777					return result, nil
778				}
779			}
780			if runtime.ResourceChecker != nil {
781				checkResult, checkErr := runtime.ResourceChecker.Check(ctx)
782				emitResourcePreflight(emit, "pre-claim", checkResult, checkErr)
783				logResourcePreflight(runtime.Log, "pre-claim", checkResult, checkErr)
784				if checkErr != nil {
785					var resourceErr *ResourceExhaustedError
786					if errors.As(checkErr, &resourceErr) {
787						resourceResult := resourceErr.Result
788						if resourceResult.ProjectRoot == "" && len(resourceResult.RootChecks) == 0 {
789							resourceResult = checkResult
790						}
791						report := ExecuteBeadReport{
792							WorkerID:          runtime.WorkerID,
793							Harness:           harness,
794							Model:             model,
795							Status:            ExecuteBeadStatusResourceExhausted,
796							Detail:            ResourceExhaustedStopMessage,
797							Error:             resourceErr.Error(),
798							SessionID:         runtime.SessionID,
799							ResourceExhausted: &resourceResult,
800							Disrupted:         true,
801							DisruptionReason:  ReadinessSystemReasonResourceExhausted,
802							OutcomeReason:     ReadinessSystemReasonResourceExhausted,
803						}
804						result.Failures++
805						result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status
806						result.Results = append(result.Results, report)
807						setExit("ResourceExhausted", "resource_exhausted")
808						emitResourceExhausted(emit, nil, "", report, assignee, now().UTC())
809						if runtime.Log != nil {
810							_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(runtime.Log, ResourceExhaustedStopMessage)
811						}
812						return result, nil
813					}
814					setExit("Preflight", "preflight_failed")
815					return result, checkErr
816				}
817			}
818	
819			candidate, skips, ok, err := w.nextCandidate(ctx, result.Results[resultsResetIdx:], []work.Guard{complexityGuard, preclaimGuard}, runtime.LabelFilter, runtime.TargetBeadID)
820			if err != nil {
821				setExit("FatalConfig", "fatal_config")
822				return result, err
823			}
824			// Diagnostic: when nextCandidate selected a lower-priority bead while
825			// at least one higher-priority bead was skipped, emit a structured
826			// picker.priority_skip event naming each skipped bead and the reason
827			// (ddx-9d55601f AC #4). Without this surface, an operator who sees a
828			// worker claim a P2 while P0s sit in `ddx bead ready` cannot tell
829			// whether the picker has a bug, the P0s are filtered upstream, or a
830			// label filter is in play.
831			if ok {
832				emitPickerPrioritySkips(emit, candidate, skips)
833			}
834			if !ok {
835				if hasGuardSkips(skips) {
836					continue
837				}
838				result.NoReadyWork = true
839				if diag, ok := w.Store.(readyDiagnoser); ok {
840					if breakdown, bErr := diag.ReadyExecutionBreakdown(); bErr == nil {
841						result.NoReadyWorkDetail = noReadyWorkBreakdownFromLifecycle(breakdown)
842						snapshot := queueSnapshotFromLifecycle(breakdown)
843						result.QueueSnapshot = &snapshot
844					}
845				}
846				if applyStop(work.StopInput{
847					NoReadyWork: true,
848					Once:        loopMode == executeloop.ModeOnce,
849					Mode:        loopMode,
850				}) {
851					return result, nil
852				}
853				if runtime.Log != nil {
854					signature := workLogQueueSnapshotSignature(result.QueueSnapshot)
855					includeBlockers := signature != "" && signature != lastIdleQueueSignature
856					_, _ = fmt.Fprint(runtime.Log, workLog.FormatWatchIdle(idleInterval, result.QueueSnapshot, includeBlockers))
857					lastIdleQueueSignature = signature
858				}
859				wasIdle = true
860				// Watch mode treats an empty queue as idle, not terminal. --once
861				// and drain exits are classified above through work.StopCondition.
862				// Emit a transient "no_ready_work"
863				// event so server-managed workers can surface this as an idle substate
864				// (ddx-dc157075 AC #5) instead of treating it as terminal.
865				emit("loop.idle", map[string]any{
866					"reason":        "no_ready_work",
867					"idle_interval": idleInterval.String(),
868				})
869				// Surface the idle substate via the progress channel so server-side
870				// drainProgress can flip WorkerRecord.Substate to "idle" without
871				// reading the JSONL event log. Phase="loop.idle" is treated as a
872				// substate signal, not a per-bead attempt phase.
873				emitProgress(runtime.ProgressCh, ProgressEvent{
874					EventID:   "evt-" + randomProgressID(),
875					WorkerID:  runtime.WorkerID,
876					ProjectID: runtime.ProjectRoot,
877					Phase:     "loop.idle",
878					Heartbeat: true,
879					TS:        now().UTC(),
880					Message:   "no_ready_work",
881				})
882				resultsResetIdx = len(result.Results)
883				if err := sleepOrWake(ctx, idleInterval, runtime.WakeCh); err != nil {
884					if exitReason == "" {
885						applyStop(work.StopInput{ContextErr: err})
886					}
887					return result, err
888				}
889				continue
890			}
891	
892			// Found a candidate: clear any "idle" substate set on the previous
893			// no-candidate iteration (ddx-dc157075 AC #5).
894			if wasIdle && runtime.Log != nil {
895				_, _ = fmt.Fprint(runtime.Log, workLog.FormatNextReadyTransition(candidate.ID, candidate.Title))
896			}
897			wasIdle = false
898			emitProgress(runtime.ProgressCh, ProgressEvent{
899				EventID:   "evt-" + randomProgressID(),
900				WorkerID:  runtime.WorkerID,
901				ProjectID: runtime.ProjectRoot,
902				BeadID:    candidate.ID,
903				Phase:     "loop.active",
904				Heartbeat: true,
905				TS:        now().UTC(),
906			})
907	
908			if allowed, reason := complexityGuard.Allow(ctx, candidate.ID); !allowed {
909				if runtime.Log != nil && reason != "" {
910					_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "complexity gate: %s (skipping %s)\n", reason, candidate.ID)
911				}
912				continue
913			}
914			// Claim atomically before model-backed intake and lint to prevent
915			// concurrent workers from issuing duplicate model calls or appending
916			// duplicate lint events for the same bead. Terminal non-actionable
917			// outcomes unclaim and park the bead rather than proceeding to execution.
918			if err := w.Store.Claim(candidate.ID, assignee); err != nil {
919				// Another worker won the race for this bead. Emit a structured
920				// claim_race event so concurrent-worker losses are observable
921				// (ddx-9d55601f AC #4). The bead remains in `attempted` for this
922				// run; on the next iteration it will be filtered out of
923				// ReadyExecution naturally because Claim() flipped it to
924				// in_progress, so the loser keeps moving down priority order.
925				emit("picker.claim_race", map[string]any{
926					"bead_id":    candidate.ID,
927					"priority":   candidate.Priority,
928					"queue_rank": queueRankValue(candidate.Extra["queue-rank"]),
929					"reason":     err.Error(),
930				})
931				continue
932			}
933	
934			emit("bead.claimed", map[string]any{
935				"bead_id":  candidate.ID,
936				"title":    candidate.Title,
937				"assignee": assignee,
938			})
939	
940			if runtime.Log != nil {
941				workLog = workLog.WithCurrentBeadID(candidate.ID)
942				_, _ = fmt.Fprint(runtime.Log, workLog.FormatHeader(candidate.ID, candidate.Title))
943			}
944	
945			// Dangling-success recovery (ddx-2b2d114e): when we just reclaimed a
946			// stale in_progress bead, check whether a prior task_succeeded result
947			// exists with a result_rev already merged into HEAD. If so, close the
948			// bead idempotently and skip re-execution — the work is already done.
949			if candidate.Status == bead.StatusInProgress {
950				recovered, recErr := recoverDanglingSuccess(
951					w.Store, runtime.ProjectRoot, candidate.ID,
952					true, assignee, now, emit,
953				)
954				if recErr != nil {
955					if runtime.Log != nil {
956						_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "dangling-success recovery error (%s): %v\n", candidate.ID, recErr)
957					}
958					_ = commitOutcome(ctx, w.Store, candidate.ID, func() error {
959						return commitOutcomeError("recoverDanglingSuccess", assignee, result, recErr)
960					})
961					if ctx.Err() != nil {
962						return result, ctx.Err()
963					}
964					continue
965				}
966				if recovered {
967					result.Attempts++
968					result.Successes++
969					result.LastSuccessAt = now().UTC()
970					result.Results = append(result.Results, ExecuteBeadReport{
971						BeadID: candidate.ID,
972						Status: ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
973						Detail: "dangling-success recovery: bead closed idempotently (result_rev already merged)",
974					})
975					if applyStop(work.StopInput{Once: loopMode == executeloop.ModeOnce}) {
976						return result, nil
977					}
978					continue
979				}
980			}
981	
982			// Queue-level decomposition depth cap: when the bead has already been split
983			// to the configured limit, block it for operator review without invoking
984			// the classifier or splitter (docs/triage/decomposition.md §Recursion depth cap).
985			if runtime.PreClaimIntakeHook != nil && rcfg.MaxDecompositionDepth() > 0 {
986				maxDepth := rcfg.MaxDecompositionDepth()

991						Kind:      "triage-overflow",
992						Summary:   "depth cap exceeded",
993						Body:      string(body),
994						Actor:     assignee,
995						Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
996						CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
997					})

8	// PreClaimIntakeOutcome classifies the pre-claim intake decision for a bead.
9	// Only actionable_atomic proceeds directly to Claim; intake_error fail-opens.
10	type PreClaimIntakeOutcome string
11	
12	const (
13		PreClaimIntakeActionableAtomic       PreClaimIntakeOutcome = "actionable_atomic"
14		PreClaimIntakeActionableButRewritten PreClaimIntakeOutcome = "actionable_but_rewritten"
15		PreClaimIntakeTooLargeDecomposed     PreClaimIntakeOutcome = "too_large_decomposed"
16		PreClaimIntakeOperatorRequired       PreClaimIntakeOutcome = "operator_required"
17		PreClaimIntakeError                  PreClaimIntakeOutcome = "intake_error"
18	)
19	
20	// PreClaimIntakeResult is the typed decision returned by the intake hook.
21	// Detail carries the human-readable rationale or infra failure context.
22	type PreClaimIntakeResult struct {
23		Outcome      PreClaimIntakeOutcome `json:"outcome,omitempty"`
24		Reason       string                `json:"reason,omitempty"`
25		SystemReason string                `json:"system_reason,omitempty"`
26		Detail       string                `json:"detail,omitempty"`
27		Rewrite      PreClaimIntakeRewrite `json:"rewrite,omitempty"`
28		// Decomposition is populated when Outcome == too_large_decomposed and the
29		// hook produced a concrete split with children and AC map.
30		Decomposition *PreClaimDecomposition `json:"decomposition,omitempty"`
31	}
32	
33	// PreClaimDecompositionChild is one proposed child bead in a splitter result.
34	type PreClaimDecompositionChild struct {
35		Title       string   `json:"title"`
36		Description string   `json:"description"`
37		Acceptance  string   `json:"acceptance"`
38		Labels      []string `json:"labels,omitempty"`
39	}
40	
41	// ACMapEntry maps one parent acceptance criterion to child coverage.
42	// Coverage must be non-empty; use "operator_required" or "non_scope" for
43	// ACs that cannot be expressed as a child bead.
44	type ACMapEntry struct {
45		ParentAC string `json:"parent_ac"` // text of the parent AC
46		Coverage string `json:"coverage"`  // child AC text | "operator_required" | "non_scope"
47	}
48	
49	// PreClaimDecomposition is the splitter output when outcome is too_large_decomposed.
50	// Every parent AC must have a corresponding ACMap entry with non-empty Coverage;
51	// a split with missing or empty Coverage entries is lossy and blocks for operator.
52	type PreClaimDecomposition struct {
53		Children  []PreClaimDecompositionChild `json:"children"`
54		ACMap     []ACMapEntry                 `json:"ac_map"`
55		Rationale string                       `json:"rationale"`
56	}
57	
58	// isDecompositionLossy returns true if any ACMapEntry has empty Coverage,
59	// indicating the split does not fully cover all parent acceptance criteria.
60	func isDecompositionLossy(acMap []ACMapEntry) bool {
61		for _, entry := range acMap {
62			if strings.TrimSpace(entry.Coverage) == "" {
63				return true
64			}
65		}
66		return false
67	}
68	
69	// PreClaimIntakeRewrite carries intent-preserving bead edits returned by the
70	// intake hook when a bead is actionable but needs safe refinement before
71	// claim.
72	type PreClaimIntakeRewrite struct {
73		Description   string   `json:"description,omitempty"`
74		Acceptance    string   `json:"acceptance,omitempty"`
75		ChangedFields []string `json:"changed_fields,omitempty"`
76	}
77	
78	// PreClaimIntakeHook classifies a ready bead before Claim. The loop treats
79	// actionable_atomic as pass-through, skips Claim for non-atomic outcomes, and
80	// fail-opens when the hook returns an infrastructure error.
81	type PreClaimIntakeHook func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (PreClaimIntakeResult, error)
82	
83	func (r PreClaimIntakeResult) normalizedOutcome() PreClaimIntakeOutcome {
84		switch r.Outcome {
85		case "", PreClaimIntakeActionableAtomic:
86			return PreClaimIntakeActionableAtomic
87		case PreClaimIntakeActionableButRewritten, PreClaimIntakeTooLargeDecomposed,
88			PreClaimIntakeOperatorRequired, PreClaimIntakeError:
89			return r.Outcome
90		default:
91			return PreClaimIntakeError
92		}
93	}

Perfect. Now I have all the information I need. Let me create a comprehensive summary:

## Summary

I've completed a thorough analysis of the four files you requested. Here are the key findings:

---

### 1. **PostAttemptDecompositionHook** (lines 45-50, 263-290, 1650-1653)

**Definition & Purpose:**
- Type: `func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (*PreClaimDecomposition, error)`
- Invoked when a `no_changes` attempt signals `orchestrator_action: decompose` in its rationale
- Used when the bead is too large for implementation-level splitting; delegates to the queue-level orchestrator splitter (same one used at pre-claim intake)

**Handling in `handlePostAttemptDecomposition` (lines 222-290):**
1. Checks queue-level `MaxDecompositionDepth()` cap (not the implementation prompt's depth-2 cap)
2. If depth cap already exceeded, parks the bead for operator review with a `triage-overflow` event
3. Calls the hook at line 263
4. If hook returns error or nil decomposition, parks for operator review
5. Validates AC map for completeness (uses `isDecompositionLossy()` at line 272)
6. If AC map incomplete or depth-capped, parks for operator review (line 274)
7. If valid, applies the split via `applyPreClaimDecomposition()`, creates children, wires dependencies
8. Parent stays open but becomes dependency-blocked on children

**Invocation Context (lines 1650-1657):**
- Called after an attempt completes with `NoChangesRationale` containing `orchestrator_action: decompose`
- At this point the bead has already been unclaimed
- Failures park with a structured event; success continues the loop

---

### 2. **consecutive_ladder_exhaustions** (lines 1456, 3352, 3367-3373)

**Constant Definition:**
```go
const consecutiveLadderExhaustionsKey = "consecutive_ladder_exhaustions"
```

**Storage & Purpose:**
- Stored in `bead.Extra[consecutiveLadderExhaustionsKey]` as an integer
- Tracks how many consecutive **per-bead-budget-exhausted** events have occurred (TD-031 §5)
- Distinct from escalation ladder exhaustion (ladder.ErrLadderExhausted)

**Incrementing (lines 1456-1478, 3367-3373):**
- Function: `incrementConsecutiveLadderExhaustions(store, beadID)`
- Called when `isPerBeadBudgetExhaustedReport(report)` is true (line 1459)
- Reads current count via `consecutiveLadderExhaustionsValue()` which handles int/int64/float64 conversions
- Increments the counter and stores back
- No cooldown is set per ADR-024 (the bead is immediately re-claimable)

**Usage Context:**
- Per-bead budget exhaustion is different from model-gave-up scenarios
- When the bead's cost budget is exhausted, it keeps the bead in the open state for retry without a cooldown
- The counter is incremented so an auto-recovery hook (noted in comment as "sister bead ddx-63155d5c") can fire when the threshold is exceeded

---

### 3. **Ladder Exhaustion Handling** (escalation/ladder.go, execute_bead_loop.go)

**Ladder Mechanism (escalation/ladder.go):**
- `Ladder` struct holds distinct power tiers and tracks viable ones
- `Ladder.Next(actualPower)` returns strictly greater tier or an error
- Three outcomes:
  1. `(floor, nil)` - next viable tier found
  2. `(0, *NoViableProviderError{Floor: tier})` - next tier exists but no viable provider; caller bumps further via `Next(tier)`
  3. `(0, ErrLadderExhausted)` - no tier above actualPower exists

**Smart Retry Path (lines 2796-2845 in execute_bead_loop.go):**

`applyNoChangesSmartRetry()` handles `NoChangesActionKeepOpenSmartRetry`:
- Calls `nextFloorFn(actualPower)` to escalate by one tier
- **If ladder exhausted (error):** falls back to `applyNoChangesOperatorRequired()` → parks as `status=proposed`
- **If successful:** 
  - Sets bead back to `status=open`
  - Stores `TriageTierHintKey = nextFloor` in Extra (for routing to use as MinPower)
  - Sets smart-retry metadata (reason, suggested action)
  - Bead is immediately re-claimable for next attempt

**Repair Cycle Exhaustion Path (lines 2847-2875):**

`applyRepairCycleExhaustedEscalation()` handles review-cycle failures:
- Called when repair cycles are exhausted (multiple review cycles failed)
- Attempts to escalate: `nextFloorFn(actualPower)` 
- **If successful:** bead returns to `status=open` with tier hint set
- **If ladder exhausted:** parks as `status=proposed` with NeedsHumanMeta indicating "repair cycle exhausted at top tier: operator decision required"

---

### 4. **actionable_but_rewritten Path** (preclaim_intake.go, preclaim_intake_rewrite.go, execute_bead_loop.go lines 1068-1088)

**Flow:**

1. **Pre-claim intake hook returns** `PreClaimIntakeActionableButRewritten` outcome with a `PreClaimIntakeRewrite` struct containing:
   - `Description` (optional rewrite)
   - `Acceptance` (optional rewrite)
   - `ChangedFields` (which fields were rewritten)

2. **applyPreClaimIntakeRewrite() validates and applies (lines 143-198 in preclaim_intake_rewrite.go):**
   - Loads the original bead
   - Calls `validateAndApplyPreClaimIntakeRewrite()` which:
     - Normalizes the rewrite request
     - Validates `ChangedFields` only contains "description" or "acceptance"
     - **For description:** checks that all "commitments" (governing refs like FEAT-010, named tests, file:line refs, dep IDs, NON-SCOPE bullets) are preserved via `validateDescriptionReplacement()`
     - **For acceptance:** checks that all acceptance criteria are still present via `acceptancePreservesCriteria()` (allows reformatting, renumbering, splitting into numbered criteria)
     - Captures before/after snapshots with SHA256 hashes
   - Updates the bead in store with new description/acceptance
   - Records an `intake-rewritten` event with preservation evidence

3. **Success path (no error in applyPreClaimIntakeRewrite):**
   - Code does NOT `continue` - falls through to next phases
   - PreDispatchLintHook runs (line 1212)
   - Execution proceeds normally via `work.WithHeartbeat()` → `agenttry.Attempt()`
   - The rewritten bead is now processed as a normal attempt

4. **Error path:**
   - Parks the bead to `status=proposed` with `PreClaimIntakeOperatorRequired` reason
   - Emits `pre_claim_intake.blocked` event
   - Unclaims and continues to next bead (line 1082-1087)

**Preservation Rules (preclaim_intake_rewrite.go lines 85-140):**

Extractable commitments that must survive description rewrites:
- Governing artifact refs: pattern `[A-Z]{2,6}-\d{3,}` (e.g., FEAT-010, ADR-023)
- Named test functions: pattern `Test[A-Z]\w+`
- File:line references: pattern `[\w/.-]+\.go:\d+`
- Dependency IDs: pattern `ddx-[0-9a-f]{8}`
- NON-SCOPE bullets: any bullet text under a "NON-SCOPE"/"NON SCOPE" section

Acceptance criteria preservation:
- Supports multiple formats: `1.` (numeric), `AC1.` (alpha-numeric), `- / • / *` (bullet)
- Allows structural reformatting (renumbering, reordering, whitespace normalization)
- Allows splitting unstructured prose into numbered criteria
- Detects drops only when verifiable criteria are missing from the rewrite

---

### Key Constants & Metadata Keys

**Escalation/Smart Retry:**
- `TriageTierHintKey = "triage.tier_hint"` - stores the next power floor for routing
- `executeLoopSmartRetryKey = "execute-loop-smart-retry"` - boolean flag
- `executeLoopSmartRetryReasonKey = "execute-loop-smart-retry-reason"` - user-facing reason
- `executeLoopSmartRetrySuggestedActionKey = "execute-loop-smart-retry-suggested-action"` - guidance text

**Per-Bead Budget:**
- `consecutiveLadderExhaustionsKey = "consecutive_ladder_exhaustions"` - counter for budget exhaustions

**File Locations:**
- `/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-63155d5c-20260511T010641-dcc3194a/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go` - main loop logic, escalation handlers
- `/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-63155d5c-20260511T010641-dcc3194a/cli/internal/agent/escalation/ladder.go` - tier escalation algorithm
- `/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-63155d5c-20260511T010641-dcc3194a/cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake.go` - intake outcome types
- `/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-63155d5c-20260511T010641-dcc3194a/cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_rewrite.go` - rewrite validation logic

The bead has 15 ACs spanning multiple unrelated subsystems (prompt templates, execution loop, event emission, circuit breaker, budget integration, 12 test functions) and the description exceeds 8000 bytes. This is clearly too large per Step 0 criteria — I need to decompose before attempting any implementation.

data.output:
ID:       ddx-63155d5c
Title:    feat(escalation): auto-reframe + auto-decompose on persistent failure (no human-in-loop)
Type:     task
Status:   in_progress
Priority: 0
Labels:   phase:6, area:agent, kind:feature, spec:ADR-024, reliability
Owner:    erik
Desc:     PROBLEM
  Today, when a bead persistently fails (ladder exhausted on
  multiple drain cycles, repair cycles exhausted, or
  review_spec_gap / review_too_large fires), the only path is
  status=proposed → operator action.

  ADR-024 P5 (no human-in-the-loop, sister bead rank 19) requires
  automatic recovery: before parking to proposed, the system must
  attempt automatic reframe (rewrite description + AC) AND/OR
  decompose (split into child beads). Only if both fail does the
  bead reach proposed.

  Today only intake-time reframe (PreClaimIntakeActionableButRewritten
  in cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake.go) can rewrite the bead.
  Post-failure reframe does not exist. Decomposition only fires
  for review_too_large, not for "implementer keeps failing"
  patterns.

CURRENT STATE
  - cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake.go: intake can apply
    actionable_but_rewritten edits. ONE-shot, before claim.
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go has hooks like
    PostAttemptDecompositionHook for review_too_large but NOT a
    general post-ladder-exhaustion hook.
  - status=proposed is the default escape hatch for spec gaps,
    too-large, ladder-exhausted, repair-cycle-exhausted.
  - No consecutive_ladder_exhaustions counter; the system can't
    distinguish "first ladder exhaustion" from "third".
  - No reframer or decomposer prompt templates exist.

PROPOSED FIX
  Add a new post-failure recovery pipeline. After ladder exhaustion
  triggers consecutive_ladder_exhaustions >= 2 (per TD-031 §6 new
  field, sister bead rank 19):

  1. Determine the failure class:
     - review_spec_gap, review_missing_acceptance → REFRAME path.
     - review_too_large or repeated execution_failed at top tier →
       DECOMPOSE path.
     - Other persistent execution_failed → REFRAME first, then if
       reframe doesn't help on next cycle, DECOMPOSE.

  2. Operator opt-out: if bead has label "recovery:manual",
     skip auto-recovery entirely and park to status=proposed
     (matches ADR-024 Operator escape hatches subsection).

  3. REFRAME path:
     - Add prompt template at library/prompts/agents/reframer.md
       — defines the reframer role's input contract (bead body +
       failure history) and output contract (structured edits to
       description and AC).
     - Dispatch a strong-tier (smart+) agent with role=reframer.
     - Apply the edits via the same actionable_but_rewritten path

Create a new bead

Usage:
  ddx bead create <title> [flags]

Flags:
      --acceptance string    Acceptance criteria
      --description string   Description
  -h, --help                 help for create
      --labels string        Comma-separated labels
      --parent string        Parent bead ID
      --priority int         Priority (0=highest, 4=lowest) (default 2)

Global Flags:
      --config string              config file (default is $HOME/.ddx.yml)
      --library-base-path string   override path for DDx library location

input.command:
ddx bead create \
  "feat(escalation): add PostLadderExhaustionHook type, wiring, routing, and recovery:manual bypass" \
  --parent ddx-63155d5c \
  --labels "phase:6,area:agent,kind:feature,spec:ADR-024,reliability" \
  --description "$(cat <<'EOF'
PROBLEM
  execute_bead_loop.go increments consecutiveLadderExhaustionsKey when per-bead
  budget exhausts (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1459), but when the
  counter reaches >= 2 there is no PostLadderExhaustionHook to dispatch
  auto-recovery. The recovery:manual label opt-out does not exist. Failure-class
  routing (spec_gap → REFRAME; too_large → DECOMPOSE; persistent execution_failed
  → REFRAME-first) is also absent.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1459 calls
  incrementConsecutiveLadderExhaustions but the comment marks the subsequent
  hook as "sister bead ddx-63155d5c" which was never implemented.
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2847 (applyRepairCycleExhaustedEscalation)
  and lines 2796-2845 (applyNoChangesSmartRetry) both fall to status=proposed
  when ladder is exhausted, with no auto-recovery attempted.

PROPOSED FIX
  1. Add PostLadderExhaustionHook type + RecoveryFailureClass and RecoveryPath
     enums in a new file cli/internal/agent/recovery_hook.go.
  2. Add PostLadderExhaustionHook field to ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime.
  3. Wire hook invocation in execute_bead_loop.go immediately after the
     consecutiveLadderExhaustionsKey counter reaches >= 2:
     - Read label slice; if "recovery:manual" present, skip hook and park proposed.
     - Determine failure class from the last attempt's stop condition.
     - Call hook (nil hook or not-implemented stub → fall through to proposed).
  4. Stub hook implementations return PostLadderExhaustionResult{attempted:false}.
  5. Two unit tests in cli/internal/agent/recovery_hook_test.go.

NON-SCOPE
  Actual reframer/decomposer agent dispatch (child beads 2 and 3).
  Circuit-breaker cost cap (child bead 4).
  Prompt templates (child beads 2 and 3).
EOF
)" \
  --acceptance "$(cat <<'EOF'
1. cli/internal/agent/recovery_hook.go exists and defines: PostLadderExhaustionHook function type with signature func(ctx context.Context, beadID string, failureClass RecoveryFailureClass) (*PostLadderExhaustionResult, error); RecoveryFailureClass enum (SpecGap, TooLarge, PersistentExecutionFailed); RecoveryPath enum (Reframe, Decompose); PostLadderExhaustionResult struct with fields: attempted bool, succeeded bool, path RecoveryPath, costUSD float64.
2. ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime in execute_bead_loop.go has a PostLadderExhaustionHook field of the new type.
3. Hook fires in execute_bead_loop.go when consecutiveLadderExhaustionsValue(bead) >= 2 (immediately after the increment at the per-bead-budget-exhausted path, line ~1459).
4. If bead has label "recovery:manual", hook invocation is skipped and bead parks to status=proposed with a NeedsHumanMeta reason of "recovery:manual label set".
5. Failure class is derived from the last attempt outcome: review_spec_gap or review_missing_acceptance → SpecGap; review_too_large → TooLarge; all other persistent failures → PersistentExecutionFailed.
6. When PostLadderExhaustionHook is nil or returns (nil result, nil error) or attempted=false, execution falls through to the existing status=proposed path unchanged.
7. TestConsecutiveLadderExhaustionsCounter passes: verifies counter increments on per-bead-budget-exhausted and hook fires at threshold 2.
8. TestRecoveryManualLabel_SkipsAutoRecovery passes: verifies bead with label "recovery:manual" parks to proposed without invoking the hook.
9. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green.
10. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
EOF
)" 2>&1

PROBLEM

  SpecGap and PersistentExecutionFailed failure classes to a REFRAME path, but no
  reframer prompt template and no reframer dispatch logic exist. When the hook
  fires for these classes, it must dispatch a strong-tier agent to rewrite the
  bead's description and AC, then apply those edits via the existing
  actionable_but_rewritten path in cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_rewrite.go.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go has no reframer dispatch code.
  library/prompts/agents/reframer.md does not exist.
  The intake actionable_but_rewritten apply path exists but is only called
  from pre-claim intake (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1068-1088);
  it is not wired into the post-exhaustion recovery hook.

PROPOSED FIX
  1. Add library/prompts/agents/reframer.md defining:
     - Input contract: original bead body (description + AC) and failure history
       (last N attempt summaries, stop conditions, review verdicts).
     - Output contract: JSON with fields description (string|null) and acceptance
       (string|null); null means "leave unchanged".
     - Role guidance: preserve governing refs, named tests, file:line refs, deps.
  2. Add reframer dispatch in cli/internal/agent/recovery_reframe.go:
     - runReframer(ctx, store, executor, beadID, failureHistory) → ReframeResult
     - Dispatches a smart+ tier agent using harness determined by ExecutorConfig.
     - Parses structured JSON output; validates it contains at least one non-null field.
     - Calls applyPreClaimIntakeRewrite() with the parsed edits.
     - On success: sets status=open, clears consecutiveLadderExhaustionsKey,
       emits reframe-applied event with cost.
  3. Failure modes explicitly handled (each returns ReframeResult{failed:true}):
     - Timeout exceeded.
     - Empty output from agent.
     - Structurally-invalid JSON (cannot parse).
     - No-op edits: both description and acceptance unchanged after normalization.
  4. Hook (from ddx-e012af90) invokes runReframer for SpecGap and
     PersistentExecutionFailed classes; on success returns PostLadderExhaustionResult
     {attempted:true, succeeded:true, path:Reframe, costUSD:...}.

NON-SCOPE
  Decompose path (child bead 3). Circuit breaker (child bead 4).
  The fallback from reframe-failure to decompose is wired in child bead 4.
EOF
)" \

input.command:
ddx bead create \
  "feat(escalation): add recovery circuit breaker, escape hatch, and integration tests" \
  --parent ddx-63155d5c \
  --labels "phase:6,area:agent,kind:feature,spec:ADR-024,reliability" \
  --description "$(cat <<'EOF'
PROBLEM
  With reframe (ddx-ca3e0d37) and decompose (ddx-60bc5e9a) paths in place, two
  gaps remain:
  1. No circuit breaker: recovery hooks can run multiple times accumulating
     unbounded cost before either succeeds or falls through to proposed.
  2. No escape hatch wiring: when BOTH reframe and decompose fail (or are
     skipped), the bead must park to status=proposed with an auto-recovery-failed
     event. PersistentExecutionFailed class must try reframe first, then if
     reframe fails, try decompose; only then escape.
  3. Per-bead budget interaction: if the per-bead budget exhausts during a
     recovery attempt, it must fall through to per_bead_budget_exhausted (not
     status=proposed).

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/recovery_hook.go (ddx-e012af90) stubs the hook without
  a cost accumulator. No --max-recovery-cost flag or config key exists.
  The two-path sequencing for PersistentExecutionFailed is not wired.

PROPOSED FIX
  1. Add circuit breaker to recovery_hook.go:
     - Track cumulative recovery costUSD across both reframe and decompose attempts.
     - If costUSD > maxRecoveryCost (default $2.00, configurable via
       ExecuteBeadLoopOptions.MaxRecoveryCostUSD), abort recovery and park to
       status=proposed with event type "auto-recovery-failed" and reason
       "circuit-breaker" plus total_cost_usd field.
  2. Wire PersistentExecutionFailed two-stage sequencing:
     - Stage 1: run reframer. If succeeds → done.
     - Stage 2: if reframer fails, run decomposer. If succeeds → done.
     - If both fail → escape hatch.
  3. Escape hatch (both fail OR circuit-breaker not triggered): park to
     status=proposed with event type "auto-recovery-failed" reason "both_failed".
  4. Budget integration: pass per-bead budget remaining into runReframer and
     runDecomposer; if budget exhaustion is detected during recovery, return
     DecomposeResult/ReframeResult{budgetExhausted:true}; hook routes to
     per_bead_budget_exhausted path instead of proposed.
  5. Add --max-recovery-cost flag to execute-loop command (and
     ExecuteBeadLoopOptions) defaulting to 2.00.
  6. Integration tests in cli/internal/agent/recovery_integration_test.go.

NON-SCOPE
  Reframe implementation (ddx-ca3e0d37). Decompose implementation (ddx-60bc5e9a).
  Hook infrastructure (ddx-e012af90).
EOF
)" \
  --acceptance "$(cat <<'EOF'
1. ExecuteBeadLoopOptions has MaxRecoveryCostUSD float64 field (default 2.00) wired to --max-recovery-cost flag on the execute-loop command.
2. Circuit breaker: when cumulative reframe+decompose costUSD > MaxRecoveryCostUSD, bead parks to status=proposed; event "auto-recovery-failed" emitted with reason="circuit-breaker" and total_cost_usd field.
3. PersistentExecutionFailed two-stage: reframe attempted first; if reframe fails, decompose attempted; if decompose also fails, escape hatch fires.
4. Escape hatch: both reframe and decompose failed → status=proposed; event "auto-recovery-failed" with reason="both_failed".
5. Budget integration: per-bead budget exhaustion during recovery routes to per_bead_budget_exhausted (not status=proposed).
6. TestReframeFailure_FallsBackToDecompose passes: PersistentExecutionFailed, reframer stub returns failed=true; verifies decomposer is called next.
7. TestBothFail_ParkProposed passes: both reframer and decomposer stubs return failed=true; verifies bead parks to proposed with auto-recovery-failed event reason="both_failed".
8. TestRecoveryEvents_StructuredOutput passes: verifies reframe-applied and decompose-applied events include costUSD field.
9. TestRecoveryCircuitBreaker_AbortsAtCostCap passes: first attempt costs $1.50, second attempt costs $0.60; verifies circuit breaker fires before second attempt exceeds cap, event reason="circuit-breaker" with total_cost_usd.
10. TestPerBeadBudgetExhausted_TriggersAutoRecovery passes: per-bead budget hits 0 during recovery; verifies per_bead_budget_exhausted path taken, NOT status=proposed.
11. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green.
12. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
EOF
)" 2>&1
